 {"id":"bd-tnsq","title":"bd cleanup fails with CHECK constraint on status/closed_at mismatch","description":"## Problem

Running bd cleanup --force fails with:

Error: failed to create tombstone for bd-okh: sqlite3: constraint failed: CHECK constraint failed: (status = 'closed') = (closed_at IS NOT NULL)

## Root Cause

The database has a CHECK constraint ensuring closed issues have closed_at set and non-closed issues do not. When bd cleanup tries to convert a closed issue to a tombstone, this constraint fails.

## Impact

- bd cleanup --force cannot complete
- 722 closed issues cannot be cleaned up
- Blocks routine maintenance

## Investigation Needed

1. Check the bd-okh record in the database
2. Determine if this is data corruption or a bug in tombstone creation

## Proposed Solutions

1. If data corruption: Add a bd doctor --fix check that repairs status/closed_at mismatches
2. If code bug: Fix the tombstone creation to properly handle the constraint

## Files to Investigate

- internal/storage/sqlite/sqlite.go - DeleteIssue / tombstone creation
- Schema CHECK constraint definition

## Acceptance Criteria

- Identify root cause (data vs code bug)
- bd cleanup --force completes successfully
- bd doctor detects status/closed_at mismatches","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-14T00:27:46.359724-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-14T00:41:54.584366-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-14T00:34:59.658781-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-tqo","title":"deletions.jsonl gets corrupted with full issue objects instead of deletion records","description":"## Bug Description

The deletions.jsonl file was found to contain full issue objects (like issues.jsonl) instead of deletion records.

### Expected Format (DeletionRecord)
```json
{"id":"bd-xxx","timestamp":"2025-...","actor":"user","reason":"deleted"}
```

### Actual Content Found
```json
{"id":"bd-03r","title":"Document deletions manifest...","description":"...","status":"closed",...}
```

## Impact
- bd sync sanitization step reads deletions.jsonl and removes any matching IDs from issues.jsonl
- With 60 full issue objects in deletions.jsonl, ALL 60 issues were incorrectly removed during sync
- This caused complete data loss of the issue database

## Root Cause (suspected)
Something wrote issues.jsonl content to deletions.jsonl. Possible causes:
- Export writing to wrong file
- File path confusion during sync
- Race condition between export and deletion tracking

## Related Issues
- bd-0b2: --no-git-history flag (just fixed)
- bd-4pv: export outputs only 1 issue after corruption  
- bd-4t7: auto-import runs during --no-auto-import

## Reproduction
Unknown - discovered during bd sync session on 2025-11-26

## Fix
Need to investigate what code path could write issue objects to deletions.jsonl","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-26T23:17:01.938931-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-26T23:25:21.445143-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-26T23:25:02.209911-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-trgb","title":"bd sync deleted non-.beads files (skills/beads/*)","description":"On 2025-12-23, commit 7b671662 (bd sync) inadvertently deleted 5 files from skills/beads/ that were added by PR #722 (commit e432fcc8). These files are outside .beads/ and should never be touched by bd sync.

Files deleted:
- skills/beads/README.md
- skills/beads/references/INTEGRATION_PATTERNS.md
- skills/beads/references/MOLECULES.md
- skills/beads/references/PATTERNS.md
- skills/beads/references/TROUBLESHOOTING.md

Investigation needed:
1. How did bd sync stage/commit files outside .beads/?
2. Was this a one-time accident or is there a systematic bug?
3. Add safeguards to prevent bd sync from touching non-.beads paths

Reported via GH#738.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-25T11:56:04.544656-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T12:05:31.785355-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T12:05:31.785355-08:00"}
